hypotheses: COVID-19 pandemic creates distinct psychological threats perceived as either realistic or symbolic, affecting compliance with public health measures.
claim.hypotheses_location: Summary of studies, between studies 1 and 3
claim.statement: COVID-19 pandemic creates distinct psychological threats perceived as either realistic or symbolic, affecting compliance with public health measures.

data.wave_or_subset: Participants recruited in March 19-20, 2020 with some pre-COVID measures from October 2019
data.notes: Limitations in generalizability due to online American samples; reliant on self-reported measures

method.description: The study developed and validated a scale to assess COVID-19 related threats through surveys and confirmatory factor analysis.
steps: Performed factor analyses on threat scale.
Tested scale's validity with psychological and public health behaviors.
method.models: Confirmatory Factor Analysis, Structural Equation Modeling
method.outcome_variable: Perceived threat level, psychological distress, public health compliance

results.summary: Realistic threats increase anxiety and public health compliance; symbolic threats influence avoidance behavior and social identity affirmation.
